info.title: CRW REST API
info.description: Firecrawl-compatible web scraping, crawling, mapping, search, and extraction API. Hosted at https://api.fastcrw.com; self-host with `docker run -p 3000:3000 ghcr.io/us/crw:latest`.

tags.name: search
tags.description: Web search via our own search backend (self-hosted) or managed (hosted)

tags.name: extract
tags.description: Structured JSON extraction via scrape + jsonOptions

tags.name: change-tracking
tags.description: Stateless diff of a scrape against a caller-supplied previous snapshot (monitor primitive)

post.summary: Search the web and optionally scrape results

get.summary: Get crawl job status and results

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Crawl job id returned by POST /v1/crawl

post.summary: Start an async batch scrape over an explicit URL list

get.summary: Get batch job status and results

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Batch job id returned by POST /v1/batch/scrape

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Batch job id returned by POST /v1/batch/scrape

post.summary: Diff current scrape content against a previous snapshot
post.description: Stateless: opencore stores nothing — the caller supplies the `previous` snapshot. Accepts a single body or a `batch` array (presence of `batch` selects batch mode). The crawl-path workhorse for monitors.

200.description: Diff result (object for single, array for batch) wrapped in the standard envelope

get.description: Returns what this opencore instance actually supports: LLM providers, output formats, search features, screenshot/renderer availability, document parsers and enforced limits. Every boolean is derived from the real build features and effective config — a capability is true only when the instance can perform it for a request that supplies no extra credentials. SaaS frontends call this on boot to validate a minimum capability set before sending traffic.

200.description: Capability report for this opencore instance

supportsBaseUrl.description: The LLM dispatcher accepts a custom baseUrl, but only as part of a BYOK request: the per-request LLM config that carries it is only built when the request also supplies llmApiKey. Sending baseUrl without a key leaves the server's own endpoint in force (the server never points its own key at a caller-chosen host). POST /v1/extract rejects baseUrl outright — configure [extraction.llm.base_url] server-side for that route. Build-invariant: no config turns this off.

serverKeyConfigured.description: True when a server-side LLM API key is present. False on BYOK-only deploys (CRW_DISABLE_SERVER_LLM_KEY=1), where a self-hosted instance still accepts a per-request llmApiKey. The managed cloud does not take a caller-supplied key.

maxConcurrency.description: Server-configured LLM fan-out cap; 0 when no server LLM config is present

requireByokHeader.description: Header name required on LLM-touching requests; omitted when no header guard is configured

supported.description: Output formats this build/config can actually produce. `screenshot` is listed only when a screenshot-capable renderer (chrome / chrome_proxy / playwright) is compiled in and configured.

llmRequired.description: Formats that additionally need an LLM: a server key (llm.serverKeyConfigured) or a per-request llmApiKey. Requesting them without one is a hard error, never a silent downgrade. When llm.requireByokHeader is set the server key is NOT sufficient: the scrape and extract paths reject these formats unless the request carries llmApiKey.

changeTrackingModesLlmRequired.description: Change-tracking modes that need an LLM. gitDiff is deterministic; json mode calls an LLM.

supported.description: POST /v1/search is usable: [search].enabled and search.searxng_url are set. Configured, not health-probed.

answer.description: Answer synthesis works WITHOUT a caller-supplied LLM key (search configured AND a server LLM key present). When false but supported is true, a self-hosted instance still answers a request that carries llmApiKey; the managed cloud does not take a caller-supplied key.

supported.description: A screenshot-capable renderer (chrome / chrome_proxy / playwright) is compiled in AND configured. LightPanda and Camoufox cannot capture, so an instance with only those reports false and the scrape path fails closed on a screenshot request.

fullPage.description: Full-page capture (screenshot@fullPage / screenshotFullPage). Same gate as supported.

available.description: JS renderer tiers this instance constructed, in fallback order — reflects both the build features and the config. These are the values the per-request `renderer` pin accepts.

mode.description: Effective [renderer].mode. Note chrome_proxy is a renderer TIER (it can appear in renderers.available) but not a mode.

supported.description: POST /v1/extract works for a request that carries no credentials of its own. The route is ALWAYS mounted, but it needs an LLM: false means a server LLM key is absent, or llm.requireByokHeader is set (which makes the server key insufficient). false does NOT mean 'not implemented' — a request carrying llmApiKey still extracts on a self-hosted instance.

maxUrls.description: Max URLs accepted per extract request (crawler.max_extract_urls).

perFieldAttribution.description: Per-field `basis` attribution. This build ships `basis`, so it reports true; the flag reflects build capability, not whether an LLM is configured.

endpoint.description: Upload path to join onto your API base. /v2/parse is the one path served by every surface: the engine mounts it at the root and under /firecrawl, and the hosted API proxies /v1/* and /v2/* only.

maxBytes.description: The ENFORCED body cap ([document].max_upload_bytes, clamped by the hard ceiling) — the same value the body-limit layer applies.

searchDefaultLimit.description: Default /v1/search limit when the request omits it.

maxUploadBytes.description: Enforced upload cap on the parse endpoint, in bytes.

post.summary: Start an async multi-URL structured extraction job
post.description: Native extraction. Returns a fixed-cardinality per-URL array via GET /v1/extract/{id}. At least one of `prompt` or `schema` is required. SDK async starters send `Prefer: respond-async` for managed/self-hosted parity.

parameters.name: Prefer
parameters.description: Request the canonical async lifecycle across managed and self-hosted deployments.

get.summary: Get extract job status and per-URL results

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Extract job id returned by POST /v1/extract

delete.description: Idempotent. Processing becomes cancelling while a claimed URL settles, then cancelled. Completed, failed, and cancelled states are immutable.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Extract job id returned by POST /v1/extract

200.description: Canonical job status + ordered per-URL results

bearerAuth.description: Hosted only. Get a key at https://fastcrw.com/register.

description.description: Alias of snippet (same value, kept for Firecrawl response-shape parity)

FieldStatus.description: How well an extracted field is attributed to its source. Honest: a field whose attribution could not be verified is 'unverified'/'unsupported' rather than given a fabricated citation.

EvidenceCitation.description: A citation backing an extracted value. Every field is server-produced; never a model-supplied url, hash or excerpt.

excerpt.description: Verbatim span from the canonical source text. Absent when the span could not be established (status 'unverified').

sourceHash.description: 'sha256:<hex>' of the exact text sent to the model.

sourceTextKind.description: Which text sourceHash covers. 'llmInput' for extraction basis (the truncated markdown sent to the model).

Basis.description: Per-field extraction evidence. Emitted only for top-level scalar schema properties, and only when the request set basis:true. citations is empty exactly when status is 'unsupported' or 'notFound'; value is null exactly when status is 'notFound'.

value.description: The schema-validated value; null when status is 'notFound'. Never rewritten to the model's claim.

BasisWarning.description: A coded reason a field's attribution was downgraded. Closed, crw-owned code set; never upstream text.

onlyMainContent.description: Strip navigation, ads, and boilerplate; keep main article content only

renderJs.description: null = auto-detect, true = force JS rendering, false = skip JS rendering

waitFor.description: Milliseconds to wait after JS rendering before extracting content

includeTags.description: CSS selectors / HTML tags to include in extraction

excludeTags.description: CSS selectors / HTML tags to remove before extraction

jsonSchema.description: JSON Schema describing the structured output shape for the 'json' format

basis.description: Return per-field evidence alongside the 'json' format: each top-level scalar schema property comes back as a Basis with an honest status. Requires a JSON schema; off by default, and when off the request sent to the model is unchanged.

headers.description: Custom HTTP request headers forwarded to the target URL

cssSelector.description: CSS selector to narrow content extraction to a specific element

xpath.description: XPath expression to narrow content extraction to a specific element

chunkStrategy.description: Strategy for chunking the extracted markdown

query.description: Query string for BM25/cosine chunk filtering (used with chunkStrategy)

filterMode.description: Filtering algorithm to rank chunks against query

topK.description: Number of top chunks to return after filtering (default: 5)

proxy.description: Per-request proxy URL (http/https/socks5). Overrides global config.

proxyList.description: Per-request proxy pool to rotate among (BYOP). Takes precedence over proxy and server config.

country.description: 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for residential proxy egress (e.g. 'us', 'gb')

stealth.description: Override stealth mode for this request (null = use global config)

actions.description: Unsupported Firecrawl parameter — returns a clear error if supplied

llmApiKey.description: BYOK: LLM provider API key for structured extraction (overrides server key)

baseUrl.description: Per-request LLM base URL override for OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. https://api.deepseek.com/v1)

summaryPrompt.description: User-supplied instructions appended to the summary system prompt (capped at 500 chars)

maxContentChars.description: Maximum bytes of scraped content sent to LLM for 'summary' format (capped at 200 KB)

renderer.description: Pin this request to a specific renderer. Non-auto implies renderJs:true unless renderJs:false is set.

deadlineMs.description: End-to-end deadline budget in milliseconds (default: 8000, max: 60000)

debug.description: When true, response includes debugExtraction trace with every candidate the extractor considered

changeTracking.description: Activated when formats includes 'changeTracking'. The format entry is the plain string; options ride on this sibling field.

goal.description: Plain-language monitor goal for the meaningful-change judge

judgeEnabled.description: Run the LLM meaningful-change judge on a changed page (requires goal)

parsers.description: Document parser directives. Omit = auto-parse PDFs. Empty array = disable parsing.

basis.description: Per-field evidence for the 'json' format; present only when the request set basis:true.

llmInputHash.description: 'sha256:'-prefixed hash of the canonical text sent to the extraction LLM. The independent record a consumer checks a citation's sourceHash against, so the check is not circular.

schema.description: JSON schema for the extracted shape

maxDepth.description: Maximum link-follow depth from the start URL

crawlFallback.description: Fall back to BFS crawl when sitemap is missing or sparse

ExtractRequest.description: At least one of `prompt` or `schema` must be present.

schema.description: JSON Schema constraining the extracted output.

basis.description: Return per-field evidence: each result carries a 'basis' array, one entry per top-level scalar schema property. Requires 'schema'. Reported by GET /v1/capabilities (extract.perFieldAttribution).

modes.description: Diff surfaces. gitDiff = markdown unified diff + AST; json = per-field diff; both = mixed.

schema.description: JSON schema of tracked fields (json/mixed mode)

tag.description: Opaque caller tag echoed on the result

contentType.description: MIME type; non-text content is hashed, not diffed

contentHash.description: Mode-aware hash; persist + supply on the next check

json.description: Parse-diff AST (gitDiff-only) OR per-field path map {previous,current} (json/mixed)

status.description: Per-page status. Set-level new/removed are computed by the caller's reconciler.

firstObservation.description: True when no previous was supplied — caller maps to 'new'

ChangeTrackingDiffRequest.description: Single body (provide 'current') OR batch (provide 'batch'). Top-level modes/schema/prompt/contentType act as shared defaults in batch mode.

ChunkStrategy.description: Strategy for chunking extracted markdown before query-based filtering

type.description: Chunking algorithm: sentence = split on .!?, regex = split on pattern, topic = split on markdown headings

maxChars.description: Maximum characters per chunk (merges short chunks up to this limit)

pattern.description: Regex pattern to split on (required when type = 'regex')

schema.description: JSON Schema for structured extraction. Used as jsonSchema when jsonSchema is not set directly.

ParserSpec.description: Document parser directive. Accepts string form ('pdf') or object form.

mode.description: Parsing strategy (auto = text-first; ocr degrades to text extraction with a warning)

maxPages.description: Optional cap on the number of pages to parse

urls.description: Count of URLs enqueued for fetch (preflight-failed URLs appear in results, not here).

data.description: Extracted JSON object (present when status is completed). Untouched cancelled slots omit data, error, usage, and basis.

basis.description: Per-field evidence for this URL; present only when the request set basis:true.

llmInputHash.description: 'sha256:'-prefixed hash of the canonical text sent to the extraction LLM.

results.description: Fixed-cardinality per-URL slots in original request order. Pending slots are processing; untouched terminal slots are cancelled.

error.description: Job-level error, set only when every URL failed.

urls.description: URLs to scrape. Each is SSRF-validated; invalid entries are reported in invalidUrls.

ignoreInvalidUrls.description: When false, a single invalid URL rejects the whole submit with 400.

maxConcurrency.description: How many URLs the job scrapes concurrently. Clamped server-side to crawler.max_batch_concurrency.

id.description: Batch job id; poll with GET /v1/batch/scrape/{id}

invalidUrls.description: Submitted URLs rejected by parsing or the SSRF guard, verbatim.

Unauthorized.description: Missing or invalid bearer token (hosted only)

SearchDisabled.description: Search is disabled in self-hosted config (error: search_disabled)
